items.title: 'Couldn't do it in Britain': Why Meghan and Harry's Australia tour is an 'experiment'
items.summary: The four-day visit could be mistaken for a royal tour, but is actually a private trip considered more commercial in nature.

items.title: China says Hormuz blockade 'irresponsible'; Trump hints at peace talks resuming

items.title: Damning ombudsman's report finds disabled brothers suffered after health department failures
items.summary: The brothers, who were admitted to hospital and treated for severe malnutrition, are now in state care.

items.title: Zelenskyy says US has 'no time for Ukraine' as Iran peace focus keeps negotiators busy
items.summary: Zelenskyy pointed to the issue of deliveries of US arms to Ukraine, saying it had become "a big problem".

items.title: Qantas and Virgin react to global fuel supply shortages — but one is less concerned
items.summary: Qantas also says that demand for services to Europe is growing, as travellers avoid Middle East routes.

items.title: From Jesus imagery to a 'holy war': Does Donald Trump really think he's divine?
items.summary: There has been fierce backlash to Donald Trump's depiction of himself as a Jesus-like figure.

items.title: Roblox launches accounts for children and teens as grooming concerns grow
items.summary: The new accounts come months after the Australian government put the video game giant on notice over numerous child grooming reports.

items.title: Man charged with using newly banned phrases in Queensland tries to argue law 'insane'

items.title: Iran remains defiant on Hormuz blockade, Israel and Lebanon prepare for peace talks

items.title: Viktor Orbán has been defeated. What does it mean for the rest of the world?
items.summary: Viktor Orbán had such an outsized global influence that his crushing electoral defeat is resounding far and wide.

items.summary: Charging ongoing fees for transit through the Strait of Hormuz would likely trigger international uproar.

items.title: Who is Péter Magyar, Hungary's new prime minister?
items.summary: Railing against the Orbán regime helped his popularity explode. Now, he may be a problem for Trump and Putin.

items.title: What your dreams could be telling you about your Alzheimer's risk
items.summary: Frequent nightmares, or the inability to recall your dreams at all, could be early signs of Alzheimer's disease.

items.summary: The United States president told reporters that he is "not a big fan" of Pope Leo XIV.

items.title: Inside Australia's push to build a sovereign missile industry
items.summary: As Australia ramps up missile production, experts say supply chain gaps and export challenges could limit its independence.

items.title: Yu went by a different name to seem more Australian, but it backfired
items.summary: More than half of Australia's residents or their parents were born overseas, but many still find having a non-English-sounding name hard.

items.title: 'Selfless, courageous' heroes of Bondi Junction stabbing honoured
items.summary: Two years after a mass stabbing at a Sydney shopping centre, eight individuals have been recognised for their bravery.

items.title: India grieves as legendary Bollywood singer Asha Bhosle dies age 92
items.summary: Asha Bhosle, who recorded more than 12,000 songs in multiple Indian languages over a seven‑decade career, has died in Mumbai.

items.title: 'Europe's heart is beating stronger': Hungary's Orbán concedes landmark defeat
items.summary: European Union leader Ursula von der Leyen hailed the landslide victory by the Hungarian opposition as a move towards Europe.

items.title: Better than Bolt: Gout Gout smashes 20-second barrier in 200m sprint, beats own record
items.summary: The 18-year-old has become the first Australian to legally break the 20-second barrier over 200m after he recorded a time of 19.67.

items.title: Government considering further economic relief as motorists urged to reduce fuel use
items.summary: Transport Minister Catherine King has warned that even if the Strait of Hormuz opens soon, there's "a long tail" to the fuel crisis.

items.title: No deal: JD Vance leaves Pakistan after US and Iran fail to reach peace agreement
items.summary: Foreign Minister Penny Wong said it was "disappointing" that a deal had not been reached, urging the US and Iran to return to negotiations.

items.title: 'Beaten' and 'subject to mock executions': How protesters are being forgotten in war
